arguments.task: Open official Intuit developer pages for QuickBooks. Determine: a one-line product/API description; all documented authentication methods for QuickBooks APIs from first-party docs; access tier/self-serve vs gated using official pricing or signup/docs pages; API surface type (REST, GraphQL, Both, or None) and breadth; whether an official or existing MCP server exists from first-party pages if documented; rate limit notes; and buildability today. Only use official Intuit/QuickBooks pages that load in browser. Return concise findings with the exact canonical URLs used as evidence.

input.thought: Browse official Intuit developer documentation to extract supported structured fields and canonical evidence URLs.

raw_result.description: Intuit QuickBooks provides developer APIs for integrating accounting and business data with QuickBooks Online.
raw_result.auth_notes: The gathered official Intuit developer pages were within the QuickBooks Online developer documentation area, but the browser task stalled on JS-rendered docs pages and did not successfully extract a dedicated auth page. OAuth 2.0 is the only method supported by the evidence gathered so far from the official developer context; no first-party evidence was gathered here for API keys, personal tokens, or Basic auth.
raw_result.gating_reason: The gathered evidence did not successfully reach a readable pricing, signup, or partner-gating page. Because the official docs were JS-rendered/stalled and no explicit self-serve or partner/contact-sales language was captured, the access tier cannot be reliably determined from the collected evidence.

api_surface.breadth: The gathered official developer documentation context clearly indicates a QuickBooks Online developer API/docs surface, but the stalled JS-rendered pages prevented verification of endpoint breadth beyond a general accounting/business-data API scope.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No first-party evidence for an official MCP server was gathered from the official Intuit developer pages reached in the browser session.

raw_result.blocker: Official developer pages appeared JS-rendered/stalled during browsing, so key structured fields such as access tier and API breadth could not be fully verified from fetched page content alone.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No rate-limit documentation was successfully extracted from the gathered official pages.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official Intuit developer documentation for QuickBooks Online exists, but the pages reached during this pass appeared to rely heavily on JS rendering and the browser task looped without completing structured extraction.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Structured fields cannot be fully supported by the gathered evidence because the official QuickBooks docs pages stalled/appeared JS-rendered and did not yield readable content for access_tier, API breadth, MCP status, or full auth-method verification.
